Phase 9c shipped: ServiceAndConcernPicker rebuilt with two chip sections (routine + diagnostic with pricing); ConcernExplanationCard + DiagnosticLoadingCard new in heritage/; ClarificationQuestionCard already existed and wired now; submit-service-and-concern-picker rewritten to split picks into selected_simple_services + approved_testing_services + explanation_required_items queue; new submit-explanation Server Action fills queue head + advances to next OR diagnostic_loading; get-current-card updated for all 4 step cases; WizardSurface wires concern_explanation + diagnostic_loading (calls runDiagnosticsV2 on mount) + clarification_question; testing_service_approval is now an empty-payload no-op per redesign D2 (run-diagnostics never advances there). npm run typecheck + npm run build both clean. Pre-existing ServiceAndConcernPicker tests still type-check (they pass empty default) but their runtime assertions test the V1 behavior — Phase 17 rebuilds them.
rationale_one_paragraph: The Vercel AI SDK was being used as a state machine for a deterministic 10-step wizard, but lite/mini models couldn't reliably emit follow-up tool calls so 19 of 19 wizard cards were already being rendered client-side via a CLIENT_RENDERED_DIRECTIVES allowlist. Two of three Edge Functions were already bypassing the LLM. Net: the LLM did nothing in the main flow but the codebase paid full chat-stream lifecycle cost. This refactor moves to a row-as-truth server-state-driven wizard: each step is a Server Action that writes the row and revalidates the page; the LLM is invoked only for Step 7 diagnostic Q&A and Step 10.3 customer-notes parsing. Chat bubbles stay as decoration (deterministic templates).

context_protocol.rule_1_one_phase_per_turn: Work ONE phase per turn. Do not chain phases in the same turn even if they look small.
context_protocol.rule_2_end_of_phase: When a phase is done: (1) set its status to 'complete'; (2) set completed_at to today's ISO date; (3) advance current_phase to the next pending phase; (4) write a one-line completion note in the phase's 'completion_note' field; (5) STOP and prompt the user before continuing.
context_protocol.rule_3_read_narrowly: Read only the slice of chat-design.md relevant to the current phase (use the section_refs the phase declares). Don't reload the whole 3,278-line spec each turn.
context_protocol.rule_4_ask_in_grey_areas: When in doubt about scope, naming, or approach, ASK the user before guessing. Per .claude/rules/never-guess.md.
context_protocol.rule_5_no_regressions: /book must keep working through phases 3-14. New wizard surface lives at /book-v2 until phase 15 swaps the routes.
context_protocol.rule_6_verify_locally: Each phase must end with `npm run typecheck` passing in scheduler-app/. Tests don't need to all pass yet during phases 3-14 (the old AI-SDK-shaped tests will be migrated in phase 17).
context_protocol.rule_7_commit_per_phase: Each phase produces one git commit. User opens PRs; do not push unless asked.

user_intent_verbatim: We need to create guidelines and questionnaires.
We also need to upload the routine services and diagnostic services for the customer to choose.
There are a few diagnostic services the customer can choose and add a description for.
These will go to the diagnosis agent to make sure all questions are answered.
IT doesnt need to recommend a testing service because they already chose the service.
On the describe issue I believe we should create guidelines and questionnaires for the model to follow.
'if customer describes noise does their description answer these questions. If not return the questions that still need to be answered.'
This can be structure so the llm can send back the questions that still need to be answered and the wizard only shows those questions.

D1_routine_vs_diagnostic_split.answer: Keep both listings. Customers may overlook diagnostic services and describe the issue under a routine chip (e.g. 'check_battery'). D2_picker_layout.answer: One card, then per-diagnostic drill-down for descriptions.
D2_picker_layout.implementation_implication: Step 7.1 picker shows BOTH routine + diagnostic chips on one card (single submit). After submit, for each picked service that requires_explanation OR has concern_categories, the wizard drills into one 'tell me about your [service]' card (concern_explanation step), one at a time. After all described, diagnostic_loading runs the LLM gap-detection per description. After that, clarification_question shows the unanswered questions one at a time. new_flow: Step 7.1 service_concern_picker — ONE card. Two chip sections: Routine + Diagnostic. Submit writes selected_simple_services (all routine non-explanation picks) + sets up the diagnostic-with-explanation queue.

Step 7.2 concern_explanation — per-diagnostic-with-category card. 'Tell me about your [service name].' One card per picked-and-explanation-required service. After each submit, advance to next OR to diagnostic_loading when queue drains.

Step 7.3 diagnostic_loading — runs diagnoseConcern() per concern_explanation entry in parallel. LLM gets (category, guideline_prose, questionnaire, customer_description). Returns array of unanswered_question_ids. Aggregates across all concerns into clarification_questions_pending. Sets diagnostic_processing_complete=true. Advances to clarification_question (or appointment_type if all questions answered by descriptions).

Step 7.4 clarification_question — one card per pending question. Multiple-choice from concern_questions.options JSONB. Customer can pick OR skip ('I'm not sure'). After answer/skip, advance to next OR to second_routine_pass when queue drains.

Step 7.5 SKIPPED — no testing-service approval card because the customer already chose. (Per Chris's call: 'IT doesnt need to recommend a testing service because they already chose the service.') The picked diagnostic services already became selected_simple_services / approved_testing_services at Step 7.1.

phases.criticality_for_v2_launch_2026_05_16: RESOLVED 2026-05-16 — see completion_note above. Original concern: BLOCKS V2 launch. Per the 2026-05-16 tekmetric-api-testing investigation (see docs/scheduler/appointment-post.md 'Empirical findings'), Tekmetric's POST/PATCH API silently ignores appointmentOption in every shape we tested. All chat-driven (API-created) appointments will land in Tekmetric with appointmentOption=STAY (waiter) regardless of what the customer picked. The CURRENT classifyAppointmentType in appointments-sync derives our local appointment_type from Tekmetric's appointmentOption.code — meaning every V2 chat booking will be counted as a waiter slot in our capacity math (computeAvailableDates), even when the customer picked dropoff. This breaks 'how many waiting appointments we take' capacity gates the moment V2 ships. Must be fixed BEFORE V2 launch: rewrite classifyAppointmentType to derive appointment_type from `color` (#D01919 red = waiter, #0D4A80 navy = dropoff, #F0572A orange = tow-in, others mapped per shop convention).
phases.summary: Surfaced during Phase 9a research: a live curl against /appointments revealed two material bugs in the appointments-sync TypeScript shape AND we currently parse only a small subset of the response into the local shadow table. This phase (a) lists out every distinct enum value Tekmetric actually returns for appointmentStatus + appointmentOption.code + rideOption.code so we can categorize and write the correct CHECK constraints + classifier logic; (b) parses every field in the response into its own typed column instead of discarding fields we don't currently use; (c) adds a raw_payload JSONB column so future Tekmetric additions land verbatim without a migration; (d) NEW 2026-05-16 — replaces the appointmentOption-driven classifier with a color-driven classifier since the 2026-05-16 testing proved appointmentOption is unsettable via API. Discovered when curling https://shop.tekmetric.com/api/v1/appointments?shop=7476 returned content[0].appointmentStatus='NONE' (bare string, not the {id,name,code} object our TS interface declared) and appointmentOption.code values of 'STAY'/'DROP' (NOT the 'WAITER'/'PICKUP_DROPOFF'/'TOWED'/'NONE' our classifier+POST builder hardcodes against).

bug_1_appointmentStatus_shape.what_our_code_expects: { id: number, name: string, code: string }
bug_1_appointmentStatus_shape.impact: appointments-sync line 220 writes a.appointmentStatus?.code which is undefined on a string → defaults to literal 'NONE' for EVERY synced appointment. The local appointments table has appointment_status='NONE' for every row. Breaks appointments_date_scan_idx partial filter (NOT IN ('CANCELED','NO_SHOW') never excludes anything because nothing is ever marked CANCELED).

bug_2_appointmentOption_codes.what_tekmetric_actually_returns_on_GET: STAY | DROP | null (so far — full enum unknown)
bug_2_appointmentOption_codes.impact: classifyAppointmentType() if-ladder never fires for real data — every appointment falls through to the UTC-hour heuristic. POST send-side values may be silently mapped, rejected, or correctly accepted by Tekmetric (testing needed). appointment_option column writes the raw 'STAY'/'DROP'/null which our scheduler code reads with different value names elsewhere.
